result: I'd eliminate all forms of scarcity—food, water, energy, shelter—through ubiquitous, self-replicating tech like advanced molecular assemblers (nanotech or equivalent). No more hunger, poverty, or resource wars; humanity redirects energy to exploration, art, and discovery. Evidence from economics shows scarcity drives ~80% of conflict (per studies like those from the Uppsala Conflict Data Program), and tech roadmaps from folks like Kurzweil predict this feasibility within decades via exponential progress in AI and manufacturing. World gets weirdly utopian overnight.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
